feature: Automatic error detection in smoke tests
workflow_rationale: Adding new capability to PlaywrightRunner to capture and validate HTTP responses and console messages. This is new functionality rather than fixing a bug in existing code.

phases.name: Error Detection Implementation
phases.description: Add console error and network failure detection to PlaywrightRunner.runSingleTest()

subtasks.description: Update TestRunResult interface to include consoleErrors and networkRequests fields for error tracking
subtasks.notes: Updated TestRunResult interface to include consoleErrors?: string[] and networkRequests?: NetworkRequest[] fields. Added NetworkRequest type import from @/lib/db/schema.

subtasks.description: Add page.on() event listeners in runSingleTest() to capture console errors and network failures before navigation
subtasks.notes: Added page.on() event listeners for console errors and network failures in runSingleTest(). Listeners are attached right after page creation, before test execution, following the pattern from test-generator.ts. Committed as 4a93b17.

subtasks.description: Add error checking after test execution - throw error if any console errors or network failures detected, and include captured errors in TestRunResult
subtasks.notes: Added error checking after test execution in runner.ts. Implementation: (1) Checks consoleErrors.length and networkFailures.length after executeTestCode, (2) Throws error with detailed message listing console errors and network failures, (3) Includes consoleErrors and networkRequests in TestRunResult for both passed and failed test cases. Commit: 26d69a0

phases.name: Integration Verification
phases.description: Verify error detection works end-to-end and doesn't break existing functionality

subtasks.description: Verify implementation by checking TypeScript compilation and code structure

verification.expected: No TypeScript errors in the project

subtasks.notes: Verification completed through manual code review (TypeScript compiler not available in sandbox). Confirmed: (1) TestRunResult interface has consoleErrors and networkRequests fields, (2) NetworkRequest type imported correctly from schema, (3) page.on() listeners for console and response events attached before navigation, (4) Error checking after test execution throws with detailed message, (5) Both success and failure paths include captured errors in return values. All types properly aligned between runner.ts and schema.ts.

acceptance_criteria: Tests navigating to non-existent routes (404) fail with clear error message
Tests navigating to pages with console.error() fail with clear error message
Failed test results include captured consoleErrors array
Failed test results include captured networkRequests array with failed requests
Existing valid tests still pass (no regressions)

verification_steps.name: Manual Verification - 404 Detection
verification_steps.instructions:
1. Start dev server (pnpm dev)
2. Create a smoke test pointing to /invalid/nonexistent/path
3. Run the test
4. Verify test FAILS with network error message containing '404'

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk change that modifies core test execution logic. TypeScript compilation is critical for verification. Manual testing required to verify error detection works in the browser environment.

checks: test_results table includes console_errors and network_requests on failure

steps: Create a smoke test for an invalid route (e.g., /builds/invalid-id)
Run the test and verify it fails with 404 error
Check test result in database has network_requests populated
